answer:
Let’s evaluate each statement:

① C major and c natural minor share the chords C minor (i in minor, but not diatonic in major) and others? Actually, they share only two triads: the diminished triad on B (vii° in major, ii° in minor) and the subdominant F major (IV in major, iv in minor is F minor, so not that). Wait—C major has C, Dm, Em, F, G, Am, Bdim; c natural minor has Cm, Ddim, Eb, Fm, Gm, Ab, Bb. Only Bdim is common? That’s one, not two. So ① is false.

② The Neapolitan sixth chord in C major is Db major (first inversion); in c minor it is also Db major (first inversion). So they are identical. ② is correct.
